stages.name: Runtime doctor and configuration
stages.purpose: Validate required NCBI email, at least one LLM provider key, recommended publisher keys, and subscription unlocks before a run spends acquisition or model budget.

stages.name: Gene discovery and paper triage
stages.purpose: Expand gene aliases, discover candidate PMIDs, fetch abstracts, then filter with a fast keyword tier and a provider-aware LLM/clinical triage tier.

stages.name: Source acquisition and corpus reuse
stages.purpose: Reuse quality-gated corpus/prior-run source first, then acquire full text, supplements, and figures from PMC, publisher APIs, Unpaywall, CORE-compatible harvesters, and authenticated browser paths without downgrading usable source.

stages.name: Source QC, supplement folding, and targeted refresh
stages.purpose: Classify missing/stub/abstract-only sources, fold on-disk supplements into FULL_CONTEXT, fetch high-yield missing source when authorized, and acceptance-gated re-extract only PMIDs with a real deterministic lift.

stages.name: Variant and count extraction
stages.purpose: Choose DATA_ZONES, CLEANED, or FULL_CONTEXT source; run deterministic variant/table pre-scans and table routing; extract variants/counts/phenotypes; then apply target-gene specificity, non-human count guards, optional count outlier/classifier hygiene, and retry failure handling before writing per-PMID JSON.

stages.name: Normalization, migration, recovery, and enrichment
stages.purpose: Normalize notation, migrate extraction JSON to SQLite, run DB-observed recovery layers without gold-PMID leakage, and optionally run full-coverage walk-to-taper, carrier-count guard, and variantFeatures enrichment/quarantine.

stages.name: Scoring, dashboard, and review handoff
stages.purpose: Score canonical DBs against normalized gold inputs, decompose recall/precision/MAE/failure classes, render the overnight dashboard, and support Variant Browser review/adjudication round trips.

active_loops.title: Cold-start loop
active_loops.detail: `gvf gvf-run <GENE>` runs doctor, discovery/filtering, extraction, source QC/recovery, DB-observed recovery layers, corpus sync, and run reporting with source recovery enabled by default.

active_loops.title: Recall-refresh loop
active_loops.detail: `scripts/refresh_recall.py` fetches new supplements, bridges corpus to harvest layout, folds source, acceptance-gates targeted re-extraction, scores, and optionally surgically lands into canonical DBs.

active_loops.title: Full-coverage loop
active_loops.detail: `gvf-run --full-coverage` adds walk-to-taper priority extraction, carrier-count guard, and variantFeatures enrichment after the bounded extraction path; it remains opt-in and should be scored before promotion.

active_loops.title: Dashboard loop
active_loops.detail: The overnight automation scores only when inputs changed, updates pipeline.json and strategy.json, rebuilds docs/dashboard/index.html, and publishes only dashboard-scope files from a clean worktree.

guardrails.title: No gold leakage in default recovery
guardrails.detail: ClinVar, PubTator, source recovery, and cold-start paths default to DB-observed PMIDs; gold-assisted worklists remain diagnostic.

guardrails.title: Source never-downgrade behavior
guardrails.detail: Corpus reuse and prior-run consolidation copy only usable full text; compromised stubs fall through to fresh acquisition attempts when credentials improve.

guardrails.title: Extraction source fingerprints
guardrails.detail: Per-PMID JSON is reused only when source SHA/size still match, preventing stale abstract-only or stale full-text extractions from silently winning.

guardrails.title: Count hygiene is opt-in by policy
guardrails.detail: COUNT_GUARD_POLICY and COUNT_CLASSIFIER_POLICY default to off; flag/clear behavior must be explicitly selected and then scored for recall, counted precision, and MAE.

guardrails.title: Surgical canonical landing
guardrails.detail: `refresh_recall.py --land` backs up canonical DBs, preserves recovery-layer rows, and reverts on unique-variant, row-recall, or rows-mode MAE regression.

guardrails.title: Generated metrics stay out of git
guardrails.detail: recall_metrics, SQLite DBs, local results, credentials, and scratch work stay local; only dashboard HTML/sidecars and the dashboard builder are eligible for nightly publication.

current_bottlenecks.title: Metric bottleneck: counted extras and count semantics
current_bottlenecks.detail: The latest disagreement report is led by pipeline_only_extras and count_semantics, so precision/count attribution must be separated from source-acquisition work.

current_bottlenecks.title: Architecture bottleneck: source acquisition and folding
current_bottlenecks.detail: Missing or abstract-only source remains large enough to block the 90% target; the architecture has folding/recovery hooks, but high-yield supplements still need bounded, authorized acquisition and refresh.

current_bottlenecks.title: Architecture bottleneck: available-source underextraction
current_bottlenecks.detail: The extractor/table-router path still needs targeted exhaustion checks on PMIDs with usable source and many missing rows, rather than broad unbounded extraction reruns.

current_bottlenecks.title: Architecture bottleneck: matcher lanes
current_bottlenecks.detail: Already-extracted indels, compound rows, splice/IVS records, and structural/CNV events still need explicit matcher lanes or denominator treatment.

current_bottlenecks.title: Dashboard bottleneck: accepted baseline drift
current_bottlenecks.detail: The fresh score matches the July 7 nightly artifact but remains below docs/RECALL_STATUS.md by 2 unique variants, 1 row, and 1 PMID, so status updates should reconcile whether this is intended cleanup or regression.
